claims.comparison_population: adolescents (age subgroup) with migraine vs age/sex-matched controls without headache
claims.supporting_quote: In adolescent sub-group, median (IQR) serum magnesium levels were significantly low among the children with migraine as compared to those without migraine [2.0 (1.9, 2.1) vs 2.2 (2.0, 2.2 mg/dL); P<0.045]. We found a statistically significant association between low serum magnesium levels and the occurrence of migraine in adolescents.
